title: AOG daemon exposes unauthenticated plaintext Raft mutation RPCs because its shipped mTLS implementation is not integrated

affected_locations.label: source
affected_locations.detail: Axum routes accept caller-supplied AppendEntries, Vote, and InstallSnapshot JSON without any peer identity check.

affected_locations.label: broken_client_control
affected_locations.detail: Daemon always constructs the default WireNetwork rather than WireNetwork::with_tls.

affected_locations.label: broken_server_control
affected_locations.detail: Daemon directly merges the plaintext aog_wire router into its app; NodeTls::server_config is never applied to the listener.

affected_locations.label: configuration_reachability
affected_locations.detail: Non-loopback exposure is blocked by default but can be enabled with a single AOGD_ALLOW_INSECURE_BIND=1 opt-in; the supplied loom harness uses that opt-in.

affected_locations.label: available_but_unused_control
affected_locations.detail: Correct mutual-TLS client/server configurations exist but have no daemon integration.

source.attacker_control: any process able to reach the AOGD listener can submit serialized consensus RPCs without a node certificate

closest_control.why_incomplete: loopback does not authenticate local processes, and the supported insecure-bind override is required by the repository's multi-node harness; no application-layer peer authentication exists after exposure.

sink_broken_control.detail: attacker-controlled RPCs are passed directly to openraft append_entries, vote, or install_snapshot state-changing handlers

impact: An adjacent network or compromised local process can disrupt leader election, inject or replace replicated state via forged leader/snapshot traffic, and compromise control-plane availability and integrity.
plausibility: High for any multi-node deployment that follows the included insecure-bind harness; medium for default single-host operation because any local unprivileged process remains in reach.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: aogd selects WireNetwork::new and a plain Axum listener; the router has no middleware; all three handlers deserialize unauthenticated bytes and call Raft; repository search finds NodeTls only in its module/tests, not daemon startup.

attack_path_facts: Attacker can connect to the configured AOGD listener.
No client certificate, token, signature, or cluster-member source check is performed.
The request body is decoded as a Raft RPC.
The RPC is invoked directly against the live Raft node.
The included loom deployment enables non-loopback binding explicitly.
